BLite.

⚖️ Comparisons & Positioning

How BLite fits in the .NET embedded database landscape — and why it's unlike anything else available today.

The Embedded Database Landscape

The .NET ecosystem has several options for embedded or local data storage. Each one makes different trade-offs between performance, size, flexibility, and modernity. BLite was designed to fill a gap that none of the existing solutions cover.

Feature Comparison

FeatureBLiteLiteDBSQLite + EF CoreRealm .NETRavenDB EmbeddedAkavache
Data ModelDocument (BSON)Document (BSON)RelationalObjectDocument (JSON)Key-Value
NativeAOT / Trimming✅ Full❌⚠️ Partial❌❌❌
Reflection-Free✅ Zero❌ Heavy❌ Heavy❌❌❌
Source Generators✅ Roslyn❌❌Fody weaver❌❌
Dependencies00Many (EF Core)Native (C++)ManyReactive Ext.
Native CodeNone (pure C#)NoneSQLite native libC++ coreC++ Voron engineSQLite native lib
Spatial Indexing✅ R-Tree❌Extension❌✅❌
Change Data Capture✅ Built-in❌❌NotificationsSubscriptions❌
ACID Transactions✅✅✅✅✅❌
Async API✅ Full❌✅✅✅✅
P2P / Distributed✅ via EntglDb❌❌Atlas SyncCluster mode❌
PlatformsAny .NET.NET Framework/CoreAny .NETiOS, Android, .NETServer-sideXamarin / MAUI
LicenseMITMITMixedApache 2.0AGPL / CommercialMIT

Performance at a Glance

Measured on a realistic e-commerce CustomerOrder document (~1–2 KB). Environment: Windows 11, i7-13800H, .NET 10.0.4, BDN v0.15.8 — March 12, 2026.

Insert

OperationBLiteLiteDB 5.0.21SQLite+JSON
Single Insert (latency)115.6 μs541.5 μs 4.7x slower3,608.8 μs 31.5x slower
Batch 1000 (latency)9,113 μs16,117 μs 1.77x slower16,196 μs 1.78x slower
Batch 1000 (allocated)31,242 KB33,491 KB6,294 KB †

Read

OperationBLiteLiteDB 5.0.21SQLite+JSON
FindById (latency)3.005 μs17.327 μs 5.8x slower28.425 μs 9.5x slower
FindById (allocated)6.46 KB45.65 KB9.33 KB
Scan 1000 docs (latency)2,115 μs7,001 μs 3.3x slower6,156 μs 2.9x slower
Scan 1000 docs (allocated)5,090 KB17,295 KB 3.4x more7,803 KB

† SQLite reports minimal managed allocations — native C library memory is excluded from BenchmarkDotNet measurement. BLite is 100% managed; all allocations are captured. Full details: Benchmarks page.

What Makes BLite Unique

0
Reflection Calls
0
Dependencies
0
Native Binaries
100%
AOT Compatible

BLite is the only embedded document database for .NET that combines source-generated mappers, zero reflection, and full NativeAOT support in a pure managed library with zero external dependencies.

This means:

  • Instant startup — no runtime type scanning or mapper initialization.
  • Trimmer-safe — publish tiny self-contained binaries without worrying about reflection metadata.
  • Cross-platform without friction — no native interop, no platform-specific builds.
  • Compile-time safety — mapping errors surface at build time, not at runtime.

🌐 A Cosmos DB for Embedded Applications

Azure Cosmos DB popularized the idea of a globally distributed, multi-model document database. BLite brings the same document-oriented philosophy to the embedded world:

ConceptAzure Cosmos DBBLite
Data FormatJSON / BSONBSON
Data ModelDocument-orientedDocument-oriented
PartitioningPartition keysCollections
SchemaSchema-freeSchema-free
IndexingAutomatic + customExplicit indexes + R-Tree
TransactionsScoped to partitionFull ACID per database
Change FeedChange FeedChange Data Capture
DistributionCloud-native globalEmbedded P2P via EntglDb
DeploymentCloud serviceIn-process library
CostRU-based billingFree & open source

If you think of Cosmos DB as "a document database that scales globally in the cloud", then BLite is "a document database that runs locally with cloud-like capabilities on any device."

🔗 BLite + EntglDb = Embedded Distributed Database

On its own, BLite is a powerful single-node embedded database. When paired with EntglDb, it becomes something entirely new in the .NET ecosystem: an embedded, peer-to-peer distributed document database.

🌐 EntglDb — Peer-to-Peer Data Sync for .NET

EntglDb is a distributed data synchronization framework that provides:

  • P2P Replication — nodes sync directly without a central server.
  • Conflict Resolution — automatic CRDT-based or custom resolution strategies.
  • Offline-First — each node works independently and syncs when connected.
  • Pluggable Persistence — BLite is a first-class persistence backend.
📱 App ABLite + EntglDb
⟷
💻 App BBLite + EntglDb
⟷
🖥️ App CBLite + EntglDb
Each node runs a full BLite database locally — data syncs peer-to-peer via EntglDb

This combination enables scenarios that were previously only possible with cloud backends:

  • Multi-device sync — MAUI apps on phones, tablets, and desktops stay in sync without a server.
  • Edge computing — IoT devices replicate readings across a mesh network.
  • Collaborative apps — multiple users share data in real-time with conflict resolution.
  • Resilient architectures — no single point of failure; every node owns its data.

🎯 Target Scenarios

📱

Mobile (MAUI)

AOT-compiled MAUI apps with instant data access, no native SQLite dependency, and optional P2P sync between devices.

🌡️

IoT & Edge

Lightweight data storage on resource-constrained devices. Spatial indexing for geo-enabled sensors. Mesh sync via EntglDb.

⚙️

CLI & Background Services

Trimmed, single-file executables with embedded persistence. Zero startup overhead from reflection-free design.

🖥️

Desktop Apps

WPF, WinUI, Avalonia — any .NET desktop framework. Local-first storage with CDC for reactive UI updates.

🧪

Testing & Prototyping

In-memory mode for fast unit tests. No external process, no cleanup. Perfect for integration testing.

🏗️

Microservices

Local caching or event sourcing with ACID guarantees and zero infrastructure. EntglDb adds state replication across instances.

When to Use What

Choose BLite when…

  • You need NativeAOT or trimming.
  • You want zero dependencies and pure managed code.
  • You prefer document-oriented data over relational tables.
  • You want compile-time mapping safety.
  • You need spatial indexing or CDC built-in.
  • You want a fully async API.

Choose SQLite + EF Core when…

  • You need complex relational queries (joins, aggregates).
  • You have an existing SQL schema you must reuse.
  • You need full-text search.

Choose LiteDB when…

  • You don't need AOT and reflection is acceptable.
  • You need BsonExpression-based LINQ queries.
  • You are on .NET Framework and can't upgrade.
📦 Get Started

Ready to try BLite? Install it from NuGet:

dotnet add package BLite
dotnet add package BLite.SourceGenerators

Pair it with EntglDb for distributed scenarios:

dotnet add package EntglDb.Persistence.BLite

Check the Getting Started guide for a full walkthrough.
